TBMM TV MUHABİRİ GÜLNUR ATAŞÇİ - Aynı şekilde, öncelikleyeceğiniz sorunları da belirtirseniz...

TRT HABER MUHABİRİ BELGİN AYDIN - Toparlamak anlamında bizim için.

BAŞKAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Tabii, aslında sistematik itibarıyla, raporumuzun genel sistematiği içerisinde milletvekillerimizle de gene rapor yazım sürecinde istişare edeceğiz, gerekirse değişiklikleri gerçekleştiririz ama önce durumun tespitini, daha sonra bu konuyla ilgili bir öncelik sıralamasını yani en temel sıkıntılar nelerdir, bunların tespitini gerçekleştireceğiz.

İşte, sektördeki temel hususlardan bir tanesi, avcılık konusundan bahsedecek olursak, gene yetiştiricilikle ilgili belki balıkçı barınakları yani balıkçıların "liman" diye de ifade ettikleri balıkçı barınakları. Tabii, bunlarla ilgili bir düzenleme süreci var; Çevre ve Şehircilik Bakanlığını ilgilendiriyor imar tarafıyla, Ulaştırma Bakanlığını ilgilendiriyor altyapı ve üstyapı itibarıyla, Tarım Bakanlığını ilgilendiriyor buraların tahsisinin öncelikle kooperatifler ve üst birliklere yapılması itibarıyla ve diğer bakanlıklarla da ilgili olduğu taraflar var. Şimdi, bu bakanlıkların hepsinin yetki alanlarına giren konular var, bunların düzenlenmesi lazım. Bir de bundan sonra yapılacak barınakların durumu ile şimdiye kadar yapılmış barınakların durumlarını belki birbirinden ayırarak değerlendirmek gerekiyor çünkü fiilî bir durum var; çok çok önce inşa edilmiş ve on yıllardır kullanılan, on yıllardır işleyen belli balıkçı barınakları var. Gene bunlarla ilgili bir genel çalışma lazım. Önce bu en temel sorunları belirleyip daha sonra kademe kademe daha tali sorunlara kadar gitmek niyetindeyiz; gene, Komisyon üyelerimizle bu konuyu geçen toplantıda da istişare ettik. Tabii, bunların yanında uygulaması en hızlı olacak konuların -bunlar temel olabilir, tali olabilir- bunların rapordan sonra çok hızlı bir şekilde uygulanmasını isteyeceğiz ve bununla ilgili bunu da takip edeceğiz. Bu arada, gene, işte, derinleştirmeyle ilgili hususları ifade ettik ama aynı zamanda irtibat hâlindeyiz bakanlıklarla, bu çok acil olan yerlerde derinleştirmelerin sağlanmasıyla ilgili de devreye de girdik, tabii, ilgili milletvekillerimizle birlikte, ilgilileriyle birlikte. Dolayısıyla bu süreçte böyle bir öncelik sıralaması, önem sıralaması yapıp uygulama konusunda da bu önem sıralamasını tabii biraz önce dediğim gibi değerlendireceğiz ama önem sıralamasının yanında hangisi daha çabuk uygulanabilir bunlara bakacağız.

Akademisyenlerden alacağımız görüşler... Bir akademisyenden görüş alarak gitmiyoruz, birkaç akademisyen, uzman olarak Komisyonumuzda çalışıyor, bu konunun uzmanları; gerekmesi hâlinde de üniversitelerle, özellikle o konunun uzmanı olan isimlerden görüş talep ederek konuyu değerlendiriyoruz. Milletvekillerimizle -malumunuz her partiden milletvekilimiz var- önümüze gelen bu değerlendirmelere şu an başlamadık, başlayacağız ama değerlendirerek tabii hem bireysel çalışmalar yapılacak hem bizim, Komisyon olarak bir araya gelip ortak bir çalışma sürecimiz olacak, bunlar kapsamında değerlendireceğiz ve malum, rapor, Meclisimiz için bir araştırma komisyonu raporu ama icra açısından, bakanlıklar, yürütme açısından sadece bir yol gösterici değil, bağlayıcılık açısından o yol göstericiliğin üstünde bir bağlayıcılığı aslında var.

Bu değerlendirmeler de gene ilgili bakanlıklarla mutlaka görüşülecek. Biz, tabii, raporumuzu önce Meclis Başkanımıza sunacağız, ondan sonra da Tarım Bakanımıza da raporu sunmayı düşünüyoruz. Tarım Bakanımızla bu konudaki değerlendirmelerimiz de bittikten sonra, rapor bittikten sonra da ayrıca Komisyon olarak da bir değerlendirme toplantısı yaparız diye düşünüyoruz.

TBMM TV MUHABİRİ GÜLNUR ATAŞÇİ - Efendim, peki, herhangi bir kanun teklifi söz konusu olabilir mi yani o konuda bir düşünceniz var mı rapordan sonra?

BAŞKAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL- Tabii, önce raporumuzu hazırlamamız lazım.

TBMM TV MUHABİRİ GÜLNUR ATAŞÇİ - Ama yani gördüğünüz tespitlerden sonra...

BAŞKAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Şöyle: Raporumuzu muhtemelen, tahminî olarak mayıs haziran gibi bitiririz, tamamlarız diye düşünüyoruz bir değişiklik olmazsa.

Burada, gene, su ürünleriyle ilgili -Barış Bey de sağ olsun hatırlattı- bir yetki süreci var. Bu, su ürünleri mühendislerinin yetkisiyle ilgili bir düzenleme talebi var, böyle bir ihtiyaç da sahada var. Tabii, bunun için bir yasal düzenleme gerekiyor, bazı hususlarla ilgili yasal düzenleme gerekiyor. Mesela, "balıkçı barınakları" dedik, "imar" dedik. Orada yapılacak imar sürecinde eğer daha alt mevzuatla çözülmesi mümkün değilse belki o konuda, o konunun imarı için oraya özel ayrı bir kanuni düzenleme gerekebilir. Bunun gibi belli noktalar var, kanuni düzenleme yapılması gereken. İşte, sit alanlarıyla ilgili bir süreç çünkü balıkçı barınaklarının -Çanakkale'de bahsetmiştim- Kale Balıkçı Barınağı'nın ayrı bir durumu var, sit alanında, hiçbir şey yapamıyorlar. İşte, İstanbul'daki balıkçı barınaklarının, limanların Boğaz ön görünümüyle ilgili, imarla ilgili ayrı bir durumu söz konusu. Tabii, biraz da girift bir konu belli açılardan. Onları ayrıca çalışıp o konularla ilgili değerlendirmeler de yapalım istiyoruz.

Sınırlamalar veya düzenlemelerle ilgili talepler var. Bunların bir kısmı belki yönetmeliklerle yapılabilecek veya daha alt düzenlemelerle, mevzuatla yapılabilecek şeyler ama bazıları da kanuni düzenleme gerektiriyor; mesela, işte Su Ürünleri Kanunu'nda daha önce 2019 yılında Marmara'da trol avcılığının yasaklanmasında olduğu gibi ve bunun, bazı yasak olan şeylerin müeyyidelerinin daha netleştirilmesi açısından olduğu gibi. Çünkü biliyorsunuz daha önce Su Ürünleri Kanunu'ndaki düzenlemelerde yasak vardı ama uygulama itibarıyla Kabahatler Kanunu'na tabi olduğu için neredeyse yaptırımı yok gibiydi. Dolayısıyla onunla ilgili bir yaptırım getirilmiş oldu. Bunun gibi hususları değerlendirip, tabii sadece bakanlıklarla değil, öncesinde kanun sürecinde partilerin gruplarıyla konunun müzakere edilmesi, Meclis gündeminin dikkate alınması, konunun aciliyeti oranında grup başkanlıklarıyla da irtibata geçilip eğer bir uzlaşı olursa çok hızlı olur ama uzlaşı olmayacağını düşünmüyorum açıkçası çünkü burası uzlaşıyla kurulmuş bir komisyon; hızlı bir şekilde kanunlaştırma da yapılabilir. Bu, tabii Meclisin gündemi ve grupların pozisyonuyla alakalı da bir şey. Ona göre biz aslında temel hususları net bir şekilde ortaya koyup hızlıca çözelim istiyoruz çünkü bu sektör ihracat anlamında baktığınızda 1,7 milyar dolar ihracat yapan bir sektör ama Türkiye'deki denizler itibarıyla dört denizle, Türkiye'nin üç bir tarafıyla ilgili ve sular itibarıyla baktığınız zaman, iç suları da kattığımızda Türkiye'nin tamamında birçok kişiye ekmek kapısı olan, birçok kişiye istihdam sağlayan bir sektör.

Sadece belki balıkçıları, tekneleri görüyoruz ama işleme tesislerinde özellikle kadın istihdamı konusunda... Bunu özellikle ifade etmek istiyoruz, burada kadın milletvekillerimiz de şahit oldular, üyelerimiz de hatta kadın milletvekillerimiz de memnuniyetle karşıladılar. İşleme tesislerinde ve birçok alanda, özellikle hassasiyet gerektiren işler olduğu için -kuluçkahanelerde de ifade edildi- kadın istihdamının son derece yoğun olduğu ve yaygınlık itibarıyla da Anadolu'nun birçok yerine, ilçelere hatta köylere kadar, çok kılcal damarlara kadar uzanan bir istihdamın söz konusu olduğu bir sektör. Dolayısıyla çok önemli, avcılık anlamında, yetiştiricilik anlamında, diğer paydaşlar anlamında ve başka yapılması gereken neler varsa, sektörün önünün açılması için yapılması gereken neler varsa bunların yapılması anlamında biz Komisyon olarak iradeyi ortaya koyacağız.

Tabii, ifade ettiğimi bir kez daha ifade etmek istiyorum, özellikle çevre son derece önemli, çevre kirliliği olması hâlinde denizlerde veya iç sularda, bu sektörün olması mümkün değil. Su ürünleri sektörünü söyledik, su olmaması durumunda, işte bir kıtlık olması durumunda, bir kuraklık olması durumunda, suda kirlilik olması durumunda bu sektörün devam edebilmesi mümkün değil. Bu anlamda sektörde belki kapalı sistemleri artırarak yani suyu aldıktan sonra onu çeşitli metotlarla kendi içerisinde kullanarak devam ettirip o steril ortamı oluşturma anlamında da belki yeni bir, farklı bir yönlenmeye de doğru gidecek çünkü bunu da görüyoruz sahada.

TBMM TV MUHABİRİ GÜLNUR ATAŞÇİ - Teşekkür ederiz efendim, çok sağ olun.

BAŞKAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Evet, başka, değerli milletvekillerimizden söz almak isteyen, ifade etmek isteyen varsa son toplantımız... Kayıhan Bey, bir daha sorayım, söz almak isteyen?

VEHBİ KOÇ (Trabzon) - Başkanım, müsaadeniz olursa...
records.englishTranslation:
PRESIDENT İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Today we are holding the last meeting of our Commission. Within the scope of this meeting, I would like to share with you the work of our Commission in detail, and if our members of the press have questions, I would like us to answer them as the Commission and indicate the issues they want to be discussed in more detail.

After conducting the wiretapping, our commission carried out a very serious field study. Within the scope of these studies, the total net working time of our Commission - that is, minus the time spent on the road - is one hundred eighty-nine hours and forty-three minutes. We carried out our longest work in our Black Sea Region for thirteen hours on 9/12/2023. Then, we work in recurring periods such as twelve hours, ten hours, eleven hours, nine hours, eight hours. Within the scope of these studies, our Commission covered a total of 11 thousand kilometers in the field and over 2 thousand pages of minutes were kept in our Commission. I think these numbers are extremely valuable as they show the working pace of our Commission. I truly believe that we have carried out an extremely productive and harmonious work together with all our MPs, representing all parties. I would like to express once again that as a result of our work, we will hopefully produce a road map and a report that will provide solutions to important issues related to the sector.

Now, I would like to briefly share with you the short story of our work of one hundred eighty-nine hours and forty-three minutes - one hundred and ninety hours of straight work - and 2 thousand pages of minutes, 11 thousand kilometers of road, and what was done during this time.

Our commission met with stakeholders, especially in its work in the Parliament. Again, we first met with the General Directorate of Fisheries and Aquaculture at the Ministry of Agriculture, and listened to them about what the process is, how it is, how the issue is evaluated by the Ministry and how it is viewed by the Ministry. Then, in our 2nd Meeting, we listened to the representatives of the Aquaculture Growers Producers Central Union (SUYMERBİR), Seafood Hunters Producers Central Union (DEMBİR) and Aquaculture Cooperatives Central Union (SÜR-KOOP). In this way, we received the opinions of all stakeholders of the sector, the presidents and managers of the top organizations, about the sector and learned their expectations. Later, when we say "aquatic products" again, since water is basic, we came together with the General Directorate of State Hydraulic Works and the General Directorate of Water Management in our next meeting. We listened to their work on the process and what they would tell us about the subject.

Of course, it was mentioned many times in our meetings, when we said "fishing", we saw that fishing shelters were one of the basic issues in terms of both aquaculture and hunting, and we held a special meeting on fishing shelters; We met with ministry representatives regarding the status and situation of fishing shelters and the work carried out by ministries on this issue. Again, within the scope of this meeting, we listened to the General Directorate of Fisheries, the General Directorate of Infrastructure Investments from the Ministry of Transport and Infrastructure, and the General Directorate of Shipyards and Coastal Structures. We listened to the General Directorate of National Real Estate, the General Directorate of Spatial Planning, the General Directorate of Local Governments and, in this context, the Department of Coastal Areas, all related to the subject, under the Ministry of Environment, Urbanization and Climate Change. The General Directorate of Provincial Administration and the Coast Guard Command were heard in this context, as they were related to the issue under the Ministry of Internal Affairs. Again, since it was a study carried out by TÜBİTAK together with the Ministry of Agriculture, we also listened to TÜBİTAK officials, especially within the scope of the project on fishing shelters; We saw together that it was an extremely detailed and holistic study, and we agreed that this project of TÜBİTAK would provide us with both data and contribution during the report writing phase.

Yes, then we quickly started our field work. Our first studies took place in Izmir and Muğla between 1-3 December 2023. We started our work in the Aegean Region. We came together with the deans and academicians of all aquaculture faculties in Türkiye in Izmir, hosted by Ege University. Within the scope of the work we do here, I will give you brief information about the demands we receive and the issues that arise in the places we visit. In addition to the issues specifically stated here, we have seen that a request for a general authority law regarding aquaculture engineers has emerged, and such a request has come from our deans. Again, within the scope of our Izmir studies, we visited fish farms, hatcheries and fishing shelters in Karaburun, Urla and Güzelbahçe. Here, they expressed to us that especially the growers at sea have logistical needs regarding the work carried out on land, and that they need areas where they can dock their vehicles and ships, especially piers and ports, in order to meet this logistics.

Of course, we have heard from industry stakeholders that this issue is also relevant to the Ministry of Tourism, as the aquaculture and net cages in these seas are also tourism areas and overlap with tourism areas. There have been requests to further increase the financing provided by Ziraat Bank to breeders, producers and hunters.

Later, we visited a fishing shelter in Güllük, Muğla Milas, Güllük Bay, and in this context, farmers and farms. They expressed to us that a fishing shelter and a pier were needed here as well. Again, one of the issues expressed by the breeders to us here is that the public opinion asks "Sea fish or farm fish?" about farmed fish, such as sea bream and sea bass, fish raised in net cages and farms. They stated that there is a prejudice that causes the question, and that this prejudice does not actually have a very solid basis because they export these farmed fish to various countries of the world, and that if they do not comply with international criteria or if they have any health problems, they cannot do this export. Therefore - by the way, there was a campaign regarding fish consumption in general, a request to increase fish consumption, as well as a request to encourage farmed fish, without discrimination.

Again, we came together with our fishermen and breeders who farm in earthen ponds in the Avşar Plain in Milas. Here, unlike in the sea, we saw that granny and various other species of aquatic products are grown in earthen pools on the shore with salt water coming out of the ground. They also stated that they are mostly small family businesses, and that these businesses are smaller businesses, such as the energy cost of these businesses... They requested that these be made a little easier, such as various analyzes that are specifically requested from the Ministry of Environment and that need to be done at regular intervals, and maybe they should be done regionally - not separately for each farm. Likewise, they also stated that these fish in aquaculture go to export, that even though they are sold in the domestic market, they go through certain controls, therefore unhealthy products cannot survive in an unhealthy environment under a certain condition, since they are already fish and live, and that fish die very quickly, so they are obliged to show sensitivity to this issue, and demanded that this burden be partially taken off their shoulders.

Of course, we saw alternative species there, we saw alternative species grown. Alternative species breeding is extremely important for us and for Turkey because when we looked at it, we saw that sea bream and sea bass predominate in the market, apart from the fish produced by hunting, and recently, especially Turkish salmon, which we call "Turkish salmon", grown in the Black Sea, is predominant, and other species are not well known or cannot be grown much. We also saw shrimp, granola and other varieties in other places we went. For example, we saw that turbot cultivation was done in Çanakkale; This is an important issue, a pleasing issue. Therefore, there were requests for support in increasing their varieties.

Yes, again, we encountered this demand in many places we visited, in the fishing shelters and ports we visited, especially because the direction of the mouths of the fishermen's shelters and ports built before a certain date were wrong, and because they were built with the wrong architectural method, some of them were damaged over time due to climatic changes and various effects, and some of them were filled with sand - we will probably mention it several times while talking about the ones in the Black Sea Region. There were demands.

Again, it was stated to us that there is a zoning process and a zoning problem regarding fishing shelters, that their locations are not clear in the zoning plans, and that these also cause difficulties in this regard, and that this process needs to be resolved.

Of course, we also visited packaging facilities, feed factories and fish processing facilities. In these facilities we visited within the scope of field work, we encountered truly world-class beautiful facilities and extremely modern, extremely clean facilities; We specifically asked whether these and all other facilities are constantly checked. Again, one of the issues expressed to us there is that these should also be at certain standards because most of the fish processed here goes for export... As you know, Turkey is one of the leading suppliers of sea bream and sea bass in Europe, and they told us that it should be processed and delivered within the scope of the cold chain, in accordance with the European Union standards, without disrupting this cold chain, and that Türkiye has made significant progress in this regard and is at a certain level.

Of course, feed and fish feed is one of the important issues expressed to us in the places we visit, especially in terms of cost, both on land and at sea. It was stated to us during the process that there was a serious exchange rate risk since the feed was priced in foreign currency.

We held consultation meetings everywhere we went - which I will explain later. We didn't just meet with the people we saw in the field and where we went; We invited all stakeholders, all stakeholders in places we could not visit and in surrounding provinces, to consultation meetings. In this context, we held our consultation meetings both in Izmir and Muğla.

After completing our work in the Aegean Region, we started our work in the Black Sea Region. We started our working visit in the Black Sea between 7 and 10 December 2023. Within the scope of this visit, we carried out studies in Kastamonu, İnebolu, Sinop, Giresun, Görele, Ordu, Ünye, Terme, Samsun, Sürmene, Trabzon, Çarşıbaşı, Vakfıkebir. Our visit in İnebolu really made a serious impact in both Kastamonu and İnebolu. The stakeholders in İnebolu were very happy that our MPs and Commission members came there en masse for such a working visit, and they expressed this to us many times. On this occasion, I would like to especially thank all the MPs in the provinces we visited, whether they are members of the Commission or not, for facilitating our work within the scope of these studies. We would like to thank the official authorities, Ministry representatives and governors, along with all other stakeholders.

We met with the Chamber of Industry and Commerce and other stakeholders in İnebolu. Due to the increase in the cultivation of Turkish salmon, which we call "Turkish salmon" as we have just stated, in the region, especially in the Black Sea, and the search for new cultivation areas, and the demand for new cultivation areas, there is a serious demand in Kastamonu - our Kastamonu Deputy Fatma Hanım is here - especially for the opening of new cultivation areas in that region. Here, as in other areas and other provinces, we see that Kastamonu may be one of the provinces that will stand out in this regard in the coming period.

Later, in Sinop, we inspected the Sinop fishing port and held a consultation meeting at the Chamber of Commerce. They told us that Turkish salmon production has increased rapidly in Sinop and that this will increase further and that the potential is greater. Here, we examined the Demirci Village Fishing Shelter on site, where we received various requests for infrastructure and superstructure works related to the fishing shelter... Of course, just before our Black Sea Region field work, there was a big storm that Türkiye has encountered in recent years, which caused serious damage in our Black Sea Region and Marmara Region. Therefore, we made these visits very recently, right after the storm, and we had the opportunity to observe first-hand the damage caused by the storm in the fishing shelters, ports, boats and the region.

Our fishermen also have demands regarding the Special Consumption Tax collected from diesel fuel or fuel. They demand an evaluation of what else could be done for them in terms of more fuel. Of course, the demands of big fishermen, boats over 12 meters and boats under 12 meters may differ from each other, but there were also requests for various vehicles and hunting gear. In this context, requests regarding the depths and the reorganization of the depths came to us. Of course, we would like to express this, especially since the demand has come at this stage; Anchovy fishing is actually the backbone of the Black Sea and the fish hunting in Türkiye. The most caught fish is anchovy. Therefore, different demands came regarding anchovy sizes. In general, there were both requests to reduce the size of fish caught and requests to increase these sizes. Of course, we would like to get the opinions of academics, especially academics, about these types of contradictory demands, determine which one is correct, and as the Commission, we would like to include what is correct in our report; We want to carry out such a study because in the places we go, there are contradictory demands, just like in these fish tribes. This may be local, seasonal or for different reasons. Everyone has their own reasons, they are also in the minutes, but this is a separate evaluation matter. Therefore, as the Commission, we will act with the determination to reveal what is right.

We held a consultation meeting at Terme Fishing Shelter. We have received requests here regarding sea snail and sprat fishing. Sea snail fishing is important in the Black Sea. There was also a request that especially sprat was given to fish meal and fish oil factories and that a regulation should be made regarding this. Because the fish called "sprat" is not actually for human consumption, people do not consume sprat. Therefore, actually fish food is a fish that the fish feed on while growing up and which is the food of the fish in their own environment. Therefore, it was expressed to us that preserving this is extremely important for the continuation of the fish species and the continuation and sustainability of the process. Again, locally, especially in the Black Sea Region - we compared it with different regions - around the streams - there was a statement about the Terme Stream in Terme - it was stated that the alluviums it carries fill the fishing shelters, and new techniques and regulations regarding these fishermen's shelters were requested.

Later, we held review and consultation meetings at Ünye Port. Here, we received complaints especially about illegal trawling, demands for more fight against illegal trawling, and requests for the construction of needed fishing shelters in the surrounding area.

We held a meeting at Altınordu Kumbaşı Fisheries Cooperative in Ordu. Of course, this place is also a fishing harbor. Here, it was stated to us that the bottom of the Gülyalı, Kışlaönü, Kacalı, Kumbaşı Fishing Shelters should be dredged and the shelters should be deepened because there is a serious shallowing. Again, what should be particularly mentioned here is that due to the changes in the coastlines, renewals should be made in the distance determinations based on the coastline, because the coastline has changed due to filling or various reasons and this is a problem for them.

Again, one of the general problems expressed for the Black Sea and in our other regions is that the fish landing points should be determined specifically and more frequently, especially during the season, because the boats stated that when the fish landing points are far away during their fishing, there is a separate cost in the distant places and this slows down the process and made such a request. Again, various requests came regarding hunting times, hours of the day, net openings and net types. In general, requests were made regarding the change, shortening or extension of the prohibited period in hunting seasons. Here, of course, I would like to summarize the requests in order to use time more efficiently. Again, there are demands regarding anchovy sizes, we have just expressed these demands.

Later, at the consultation meeting we held at Görele Fishing Shelter, a request was made regarding the extension of the breakwater of the shelter. Of course, here we want to express the following: We have seen that even if the fishing shelters and ports were suitable in terms of the capacity at that time, even if they were built in excess, at this point it is no longer sufficient, it is not sufficient both in terms of logistics and physical space, therefore enlargement works or rearrangement work must be carried out within the scope of needs; The demands were also in this direction. Particularly in Görele, a review of the snail fishing requirement of 500 meters from the shore, a review of depth-related processes and depth criteria, and changes in this regard were requested.

There is another request regarding shallowing in Vakfıkebir Fishing Shelter. Since these are, of course, harbors and fishing shelters where large boats come and go, we have seen with our own eyes that there is real shallowing in certain places. We have also contacted the relevant ministries regarding this issue, but we will also evaluate this issue separately in our report. I think it will be included in our report appropriately.

Again, we inspected Çarşıbaşı Fishing Shelter and held a consultation meeting. There were also requests for a fishing shelter here.

In the province of Trabzon - in addition to all these places we visited - we invited the provinces of Rize and Artvin to Trabzon, and by inviting the stakeholders there to Trabzon, we held a consultation meeting - as in all provinces, everywhere. Here, demands came for the opening of new areas, especially in the field of cultivation. There were requests regarding the high costs of water rentals. Regarding financing, requests have been made for the cages and the fish inside to be accepted as collateral when obtaining financing from the Credit Guarantee Fund or other channels. Likewise, since we invited different non-governmental organizations and different stakeholders to our consultations, there was also a demand for determining the locations of new production facilities to be built in Trabzon, in consultation with both aquaculture cooperatives and people in the region. Regarding turbot hunting - as you know, it is an important fish in the Black Sea with a really high economic value - it was not prohibited in different countries at the time when it was prohibited, therefore, a request came for the countries around the Black Sea to make similar regulations regarding hunting in similar times, if possible.

Therefore, the issues we have talked about so far, and in fact the issues we will say from now on, have shown us that the aquaculture sector has aspects related to not only one Ministry, the Ministry of Agriculture, the General Directorate of Fisheries, but actually all Ministries. Within the scope of studies to be carried out to increase the consumption of aquatic products, it was recommended to encourage this consumption to new generations. For this reason, it was suggested that a study on this subject be carried out in schools through the Ministry of National Education; A part related to the Ministry of National Education. Now, when we look at the studies and international agreements regarding the problems faced by our fishermen in different countries, on different continents, including Africa, making similar arrangements with neighboring countries, part of it is related to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Again, when we look at the section regarding the Coast Guard, there is a section regarding the Ministry of Internal Affairs. There is a section about the Ministry of Commerce. There is a section related to the Ministry of Industry in terms of incentives given regarding cultivation and facilities. In other words, we have clearly established here in the field that the aquaculture sector has a part related to all Ministries and that it is a process related to all Ministries.

Later, as a result of our consultations in Trabzon, we completed our field studies in the Black Sea Region. These studies showed us that there are some regional differences, some regional demands, and general demands when compared to the Aegean Region. It also showed us that different provinces in the region, even different fishing shelters, and different types of hunting have different demands. Of course, in terms of recording all of these, I think our field work was extremely productive and valuable.

Then, we continued our field studies in Van on 29-30 December 2023. Of course, stakeholders related to our aquaculture, hunting or aquaculture are not only located at the seaside. Since we have inland waters and there are freshwater creatures in inland waters, there is also a serious industry in inland waters. In particular, a large part of the fishing in inland waters is carried out in Lake Van, our largest lake, especially for the pearl mullet, which is an endemic species. Therefore, we carried out our work in Van in order to see the process here on site, and to bring the pearl mullet, which is endemic to Van and lives in soda water, to the agenda once again, and to open a separate topic in our Commission within the scope of the Commission's work. As part of these studies, we visited Edremit Fishing Shelter. There we listened to the troubles of the fishermen. We saw that there was a serious water withdrawal in the shelter and in Van in general. We have observed that physical spaces have become almost unusable due to this retreat of up to a few meters. Later, we saw that before, when it was built, you could go up a few steps from the port to the boat, but now you go down 5-6 steps to the boat. This withdrawal of meters is also extremely important for the sector: "No water, no aquaculture." What we said has once again shown us that if the environment does not exist and the climate chaos continues like this, the sector may face serious problems, as in all other areas.

We examined the Engil Stream. The pearl mullet of the Engil Stream... As you know, it lives in soda water in Lake Van, but in order to breed, it goes to the breeding areas through fresh waters and streams and lays its eggs there. The pearl mullet fry that breed in fresh water then follow a certain process and follow the same path, returning to that soda water, Lake Van, and continue their lives there. Therefore, we followed this process closely. Of course, we heard from the authorities about the work done there and the work done to protect the pearl mullet during this migration, and in our interviews with the local people there, we saw that the local people are actually extremely sensitive about this issue and that they are generally aware of how important the pearl mullet is as a whole and that it is protected; This made us extremely pleased. As you know, no matter how much you try to protect it with law enforcement, it is obvious that there will be vulnerabilities in a system that the citizens do not protect, but they also stated that the citizens also protect this protection process, especially children - one of the things that was expressed to us there - is that information is given by our Ministry of National Education in schools within the scope of information activities, and children repeatedly remind their families of this, especially during the protection process and during this migration process. Therefore, this is a good situation that we want to share.

We held consultations at the Provincial Directorate of Agriculture and Forestry. Of course, our academics at Yüzüncü Yıl University made presentations on the subject, and the Provincial Directorate and the Provincial Directorate of Agriculture made presentations to us about the work done in Van. They stated that there is serious farming in Van, not only of pearl mullet but also of trout, and that the water resources are extremely productive in this sense. Of course, we have observed in the field that the added value of pearl mullet, as a sector that needs to be developed, should be increased, and that the value of this pearl mullet, which is an endemic species and has a catch limit of a certain ton, should not only be hunted and consumed, but also introduced to the whole world through cold storages, processing facilities and a special study. The stakeholders there expressed this to us. Again, demands regarding input costs were expressed in Van.

Then we started our work in the Marmara Region. We carried out our work in the Marmara Region in two parts; We carried out our field work in the provinces on the southern coast of the Marmara Sea, which we call South Marmara, on 9-11 January 2024, starting from Çanakkale. Of course, within the scope of these studies we carried out in Çanakkale, Balıkesir, Bursa, Yalova and Kocaeli, we visited an important manufacturer among Türkiye's top 500 companies, where they both told us about their investments and saw their production facilities there; Of course, we are extremely pleased to have such beautiful production facilities in our country. They seriously stated that they have a supply to both the domestic market and the foreign market through exports, and that they bring serious foreign currency to our country. We have seen that they produce and supply both domestically and abroad by producing added value with different types and different processing methods.

Of course, we came together in Çanakkale in our consultation with the cooperative representatives. Representatives from many fishing shelters such as Biga Fishing Shelter, Yeniköy Fishing Shelter, and Güneyli and Kemikliburun Fishing Shelters also came to this consultation meeting, which was hosted by the Commodity Exchange and held in the Commodity Exchange building. Then we visited Kemer Fishing Shelter in Biga. Of course, we have seen that some fishermen's shelters and ports face different processes and difficulties due to their being protected areas - and this is one of them; We met with our producers and hunters here and listened to their demands. Due to the lack of boating areas here - I do not want to list them one by one - there were demands for the expansion of the mooring areas and the depth of the fishing shelters.

Later, we held our consultations in Balıkesir and the next day we inspected the Karşıyaka, Çakıl and Kestanelik Fishing Shelters in Erdek. Of course, like some provinces in the Black Sea and Marmara, Balıkesir actually has a special place in our fishing fleet, especially in terms of fishing. One of the places where a significant part of our fleet is located is these fishing shelters we visit. It is a place where a significant percentage of the fleet is located, both in terms of boat sizes, boat fishing capacities and numbers. We met with our fishermen here. Here again, there were requests from our fishermen, similar to those made before, and I will not repeat them to avoid repetition.

The cooperatives in Lake Manyas told us that they wanted to make a regulation regarding the rental prices of Manyas lake - because as you know, Manyas is in Balıkesir - there should be a regulation in the prices and rental amounts of lake rentals, and that they would like to rent these places at more affordable prices.

There was a request to remove quotas on some fish species, such as Chinese carp. There were different demands regarding trawling. Since Balıkesir has coasts on both the Aegean and the Marmara... As you know, trawling is prohibited in the Marmara, but it is free in the Aegean, so the fishermen who came there conveyed their demands regarding different seas to us in Balıkesir.

In Bursa, we visited the fishing shelter and Kurşunlu Fisherman's Shelter in the center of Gemlik. In Bursa, it was stated to us that it is important to set a minimum price for fish as the fish find their value, to prevent the fish from being too cheap one week, and too expensive the next week if there is a storm or cannot be caught for other reasons, even if it is in the free market, and this is important for fishermen to be able to foresee their future.

Again, a request for regulation regarding fish markets was made. There were demands that a uniform regulation regarding boats over 12 meters and under 12 meters does not actually reflect the reality in the field and is not compatible with the reality in the field, so it should be rearranged.

Later, inspections were carried out in Yalova, Çınarcık Fishing Shelter, and we also carried out inspections in this context in Esenköy. Of course, in general, the demands of fishing ports or fishermen may be similar in the Marmara Region.

Various issues were expressed regarding shipyards and anchorage areas. Especially in the Marmara Region - one of the things that was expressed to us in Istanbul - is that fishermen cannot hunt due to both the Bosphorus passages and the anchorage areas related to the ports, that it is not possible for them to hunt in these areas, and they also demanded that these areas be opened to them, at least when there is no anchorage.

Then, we made a study visit in Istanbul between 19-21 January. We carried out these studies differently on the Anatolian side and the European side, and we held a consultation meeting at the Chamber of Shipping in Istanbul. Here, of course - as I have just stated - both Rumeli Feneri and Poyrazköy are places that host a significant percentage of the fishing fleet in Türkiye, a significant percentage of the hunting ships, both in the Black Sea, in the Marmara, and in fact, due to the size of the ships, in our other seas and international waters, in the waters of other countries and in other countries, where fishing vessels are moored. Therefore, their international agreements, encouraging fishing in other countries, providing financial support for hunting in other countries, ensuring and monitoring the protection of fishermen and boats going there in accordance with the agreements. They requested that they be supported in this regard in case any dispute arises. Of course, one of the issues expressed there is that in the absence of fishing, small fishermen... Of course, as you know, 90 percent of our fleet consists of small fishermen, and they take approximately 10-20 percent of the fish caught, but the remaining 10 percent carries out almost 80 percent of the fishing. Of course, we saw once again that it is not possible to produce enough fish for our provinces, especially Istanbul, Ankara and Izmir, and enough fish catch for Türkiye, without big fishermen and big boats, but while doing this, we saw that various regulations, well-founded regulations must be made and sustainability must be ensured.

Here again, they stated that there was an important request for them to reduce the length of the horse mackerel from 13 centimeters to 12 centimeters, and such a request was received. Of course, the evaluation of this belongs to our Commission. This is how we shared this with you.

Again, a request has been made regarding the season, especially regarding purse purse fishing, their species and hunting seasons, to evaluate the new conditions that have emerged as a result of the new climate chaos, new climatic conditions, and changes in fish breeding times, if any, and perhaps to review this season as well.

We visited Rumeli Feneri and Poyrazköy Fisheries Cooperative on the Anatolian Side. There are demands here one by one, there is no need to count them one by one. It can also be viewed from our commission reports.

Again, in Istanbul, we visited the Fatih Fisheries Cooperative in both Maltepe and Kocamustafapaşa, not only the places where there are big fishermen, but also the places where there are smaller fishermen, and we also met with them; They also made demands regarding what is needed to sustain the process, especially for small fishermen. They said that the ban on trawling related to the Fisheries Law, which was previously implemented in 2019 and came into force in 2020, benefited them, especially regarding bottom fish, giving the example of red snapper. Regarding red snapper, they stated that small fishermen used it to make a living - so to speak - when other fish were not available, and that this trawling ban should continue.

Again, within the scope of our consultations at the Chamber of Shipping, we talked about the insurance status of the crew working on fishing boats - of course, we have just mentioned it - and the crew over a certain number on large fishing boats and the laborers working there. A request was made regarding their insurance status and the process of paying a separate premium for them. As the Commission, we requested the opinion of the relevant ministry on this issue. We consider this to be one of the important issues.

As Vehbi Koç stated in our last meeting, in addition to making definitions such as what is a fishing boat, where is a fishing shelter, what are the powers of aquaculture engineers regarding this sector, there were also requests for the determination of their status, as fishing cannot be done where there is no crew, where there is no boat, since each of them is a ship. Therefore, I wanted to express this issue specifically.

Again, demands have been made for the aquaculture to be covered by agricultural insurance - especially for aquaculture facilities - and to compensate for the damages related to them and other damages in both hunting and aquaculture within this scope. During our consultations, there were also requests and expressions regarding the technical dimensions, that there are evaluation criteria and criteria such as the length of the boat, its gross tonnage, and its engine size, and that these should be subjected to a certain evaluation and rating, and that perhaps arrangements should be made in certain places and on certain issues. There were also demands that amateur fishermen should be registered, an amateur certificate should be issued, and that they should be inspected to see if they comply with the rules, like big fishermen or commercial fishermen. Requests came regarding anchorages, navigation routes of commercial ships and especially areas closed to hunting. There are also requests for hunting in these areas at certain periods, and there are also requests for further expansion of these areas. This evaluation is of course; Whatever is right, the truth will be decided with the help of academics, because we think that we can determine what is right with the help of scientists, scientists who know this field.

There are requests regarding depth bans. Demands regarding the reduction of depth bans, as you know, 24 meters, 18 meters in the Black Sea, lowering them, lowering them or raising them higher are also among the requests we receive. There are demands regarding BAGİS devices; This was expressed in different regions we went to, a system created by the Ministry of Agriculture for monitoring fishing boats, a system for electronic monitoring. We received requests regarding malfunctions related to this, a few issues that emerged in the new legislation, and a few problems, and we listened to them.

Of course, one of the important works we carried out in Istanbul was the fish markets we carried out between twelve and half past two at night, because the working hours of the fish markets are, as you know, from twelve to five or six in the morning at the latest. Therefore, we would like to thank our Commission members once again, we carried out the fish harvest work between those hours, until two thirty at night, and we actually heard firsthand what the process was, what the needs were, what the demands of the market tradesmen were, and what the demands of the breeders or hunters who brought their fish there were. One of the issues expressed to us there is; It was stated to us that fish distribution to the cities would not be possible without the market, therefore the arrangements of these markets should be made appropriately and correctly, and the logistics process between the fish exit points and the markets should be planned correctly.

We completed our work in the Marmara Region after these studies in Istanbul. We have observed once again that the sector representatives and stakeholders are very pleased with our on-site inspections as the Commission in the Marmara Region, as in other regions, and the fact that we go to the provinces and listen to the processes at their feet, so to speak.

Later, we carried out our last field study, covering the provinces of Kayseri, Mersin and Adana, that is, the Central Anatolia and Mediterranean Region, last week between 9-11 February 2024. Since Kayseri is a city that produces 37 percent of the baby trout production in Türkiye, it is one of the provinces that should be visited in inland waters, like other important places. Of course, maybe it should be stated on this occasion: There are places that must be visited in all our provinces, maybe we should visit more provinces, but due to the fact that the Parliament is working, the local election process, time constraints due to the agendas of politics and our Commission period being three months, four months with a one-month extension, we could not visit these provinces. But we made sure to listen to them by inviting them to the surrounding provinces we visited. We contacted them through the relevant directorates to get their opinions in writing about those we could not hear.

We visited a large incubation facility and a small family business in Pınarbaşı district of Kayseri. We held our consultation meeting at the AFAD provincial building and thus listened to the stakeholders in Kayseri.

Then, we visited the Karaduvar Fishing Shelter, the largest fishing shelter in Mersin, where the most boats are moored. We invited stakeholders from Antalya and other provinces to Mersin and held our consultation meeting with them. Again, there were requests regarding water rentals, BAGİS device and financial support. We came together with stakeholders at the consultation meeting at the Mediterranean Exporters' Association. We met with sector stakeholders and cooperative presidents from other provinces, Antalya and Isparta, and listened to their demands. Of course, shrimp fishing has an important place in the Mediterranean Region, especially in the Mersin and Adana regions, and is an important source of income and livelihood. Therefore, they had demands regarding shrimp fishing. A request has also been made regarding the evaluation and limitation of shrimp imports from abroad that may compete unfairly with these hunters or producers. Here, of course, regarding the cultivation in inland waters, our growers, especially those coming from the Antalya region and Isparta region, had requests regarding the leasing of waters and the leasing of forest areas within the scope of forestry legislation. There was a demand not only for cultivation in these areas, but also for small family businesses to be allowed to cook or eat, and to allow small restaurants to be opened in order to survive.

Later, we continued our work in Adana. Karataş Fisheries Cooperative visited the Karataş Fishing Shelter, one of the largest fishing shelters in the Mediterranean, to which we invited the fishermen in the surrounding area and in Hatay. Of course, there is a demand for shrimp in Karataş, just like in Mersin. Since the Mediterranean is slightly different from the Black Sea and our other seas in terms of fish diversity, there is no anchovy fishing there, but it also has its own valuable species, such as shrimp and other species. They demanded that they should be supported in their hunting, and that they should be given the opportunity to hunt outside the breeding season - such as sole fish - by taking into account their breeding seasons, and that this hunting season should be regulated regionally and in relation to the species... In other words, regional hunting, regional bans, regional regulations should be introduced. They shared this with us.

Of course, the colorful images of our fishing shelters also became the scene of colorful dialogues. We held the consultation meeting later. As you know, our province of Adana is a city with many bests and firsts in two areas. Regarding dalyans, the city with the highest number of dalyans in Türkiye is Adana. Therefore, special issues regarding the operation of dalyans and dalyans were expressed. Of course, Adana Çukurova region is a province with very fertile agricultural lands and abundant water, which brings with it some problems, including pesticide-based pollution, which we may not encounter as much in other places.

Again, one of the issues I would like to express in particular is that Türkiye's and Europe's first aquaculture specialized organized industrial zone will be built here; As part of our work in Adana, we also visited the area where this will be done, where we received information from the authorities, and within the scope of this information, they stated to us that the infrastructure works will start quickly in the near future. Of course, this place is extremely important for the production of alternative species, for the development of the sector and in terms of export, because there is an alternative fishery in this region, especially shrimp, and that region will be a separate production center for alternative species, perhaps for species that need temperatures above a certain temperature due to the warm water and warm weather. In this sense, we are frankly pleased to see that this and such an idea is being implemented.

During our work in Adana, within the scope of consultations - in the surrounding countries, of course, all the surrounding countries of the Mediterranean - especially when we look at the Adana and Mersin region, we talked about countries such as Syria and Egypt, and that they sometimes have an advantage in terms of hunting in international waters, and the request for international agreements and international regulations regarding this issue came to us.

So, I made a very short recap, maybe not too short for you, but an hour; Rest assured, we have made a very short recap. We, along with all our deputies in the field, listened patiently to the issues we listed as headings for hours - well, we just explained, Mr. Barış, we have one hundred eighty-nine hours and forty-three minutes of clear work - one hundred and eighty-nine hours, and sometimes even encouraged the participants of the meeting to take the floor. We listened to them almost without interruption, listening to all the points they wanted to say and recording them in the minutes. Thus, we have concluded our Commission work, field work and work in the Parliament in this sense. This is, of course, our last Commission meeting. We are now completing our working period as of the 17th. But as you know, since the Parliament will not work the day after tomorrow, we held the last official meeting of our Commission with you today, on Thursday, the last official working day.

We can take your questions, especially if you have questions about this subject or the issues I have explained.

Come on, introduce yourself, please.

TRT NEWS CORRESPONDENT BELGİN AYDIN ​​- Belgin Aydın, TRT News.

Now, the report will be prepared regarding the work of the Commission. Of course, we will see it in the report, but first of all, even though it consists of a few such items, what inferences did you get from the names you listened to, especially in terms of solution suggestions, and what will be reflected in the report? We would actually be very happy if we received a few titles.

PRESIDENT İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Like this...

TBMM TV CORRESPONDENT GÜLNUR ATAŞÇİ - I also...

PRESIDENT İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Here you go.

GNAT TV CORRESPONDENT GÜLNUR ATAŞÇİ - Likewise, if you indicate the problems you will prioritize...

TRT NEWS CORRESPONDENT BELGİN AYDIN - For us in the sense of tidying up.

CHAIRMAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Of course, in fact, systematically, within the general systematics of our report, we will also consult with our MPs during the report writing process. If necessary, we will make changes, but first we will determine the situation, then we will make a priority order on this issue, that is, what the most basic problems are.

Here, one of the basic issues in the sector, if we talk about hunting, perhaps also related to aquaculture, is fishing shelters, which fishermen also refer to as "harbours". Of course, there is a regulatory process regarding these; It concerns the Ministry of Environment and Urbanization in terms of zoning, the Ministry of Transport in terms of infrastructure and superstructure, the Ministry of Agriculture in terms of the allocation of these places primarily to cooperatives and superior unions, and other ministries. Now, all of these ministries have issues that fall under their jurisdiction, and these need to be regulated. It is also necessary to evaluate the situation of the shelters that will be built from now on and the situation of the shelters that have been built so far, separately, because there is an actual situation; There are certain fishing ports that were built long, long ago and have been used for decades and have been operating for decades. Again, a general study on these is needed. We intend to first identify these most basic problems and then gradually move on to more secondary problems; Again, we discussed this issue with our Commission members at the last meeting. Of course, in addition to these, we will want the issues that will be implemented the fastest - these may be basic or secondary - to be implemented very quickly after the report, and we will follow up on this. By the way, again, we have expressed the issues related to deepening, but we are also in contact with the ministries, we have also stepped in to ensure deepening in these very urgent areas, of course, together with our relevant MPs and relevant people. Therefore, in this process, we will make such a priority order and importance order and evaluate this importance order in terms of implementation, as I said before, but besides the importance order, we will look at which one can be implemented more quickly.

The opinions we will get from academics... We are not going there by getting an opinion from an academician, several academics work in our Commission as experts, experts on this subject; If necessary, we evaluate the issue by requesting opinions from universities and especially from experts on that subject. We have not started these evaluations with our MPs - as you know, we have MPs from every party - we will start, but by evaluating, of course, both individual studies will be carried out and we will come together as the Commission and have a joint working process, we will evaluate within the scope of these and, as you know, the report is an investigation commission report for our Parliament, but in terms of execution, ministries, executive, it is not just a guide, in terms of binding, it actually has a binding force beyond that guidance.

These evaluations will definitely be discussed with the relevant ministries. We, of course, will first present our report to the Speaker of the Parliament, and then we plan to present the report to our Minister of Agriculture. We think that after our evaluations on this issue with our Minister of Agriculture are completed, we will also hold an evaluation meeting as the Commission after the report is completed.

GNAT TV CORRESPONDENT GÜLNUR ATAŞÇİ - Sir, could there be any proposed law, do you have any thoughts on that subject after the report?

PRESIDENT İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL- Of course, we need to prepare our report first.

GNAT TV CORRESPONDENT GÜLNUR ATAŞÇİ - But after the findings you have seen...

CHAIRMAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - As follows: We think that we will probably complete our report around May or June, if there is no change.

Here again, there is an authorization process regarding aquatic products - thanks to Mr. Barış reminded us. There is a demand for regulation regarding the authority of aquaculture engineers, and such a need exists in the field. Of course, this requires a legal regulation, a legal regulation is required on some issues. For example, we said "fishing shelters", we said "zoning". If it is not possible to solve it with lower legislation during the zoning process to be carried out there, a separate legal regulation specific to that issue may be required for the zoning of that issue. There are certain points like this that require legal regulation. Here is a process related to protected areas, because fishing shelters - I mentioned in Çanakkale - Kale Fishing Shelter have a different situation, they are in protected areas, they cannot do anything. Here, there is a separate situation regarding the zoning of fishermen's shelters and ports in Istanbul regarding the Bosphorus front view. Of course, it is a somewhat complicated subject in certain respects. We also want to study them and make evaluations on those issues.

There are requests for limitations or regulations. Some of these may be things that can be done by regulations or by sub-regulations and legislation, but some of them also require legal regulation; For example, in the Fisheries Law, as in the previous ban on trawling in the Marmara in 2019, and in terms of clarifying the sanctions of some prohibited things. Because, as you know, there was a ban in the regulations of the Fisheries Law before, but since it was subject to the Misdemeanor Law in practice, it seemed to have almost no sanctions. Therefore, a sanction was imposed regarding it. Evaluating issues like this, discussing the issue not only with the ministries, but also with the groups of the parties during the legislative process, taking into account the agenda of the Parliament, contacting the group chairs in proportion to the urgency of the issue, and if there is a consensus, it would be very quick, but I do not think that there will be a consensus, frankly, because this is a commission established by consensus; It can also be enacted quickly. This, of course, is also related to the agenda of the Assembly and the positions of the groups. According to him, we actually want to present the basic issues clearly and solve them quickly, because when you look at this sector in terms of exports, it is a sector that exports 1.7 billion dollars, but when you look at the seas in Türkiye, it is related to the four seas, three sides of Türkiye and in terms of waters, when we include the inland waters, it is a sector that provides bread and butter for many people throughout Türkiye.

We only see fishermen and boats, but especially about the employment of women in processing plants... We would like to express this specifically, our female MPs also witnessed it, our members and even our female MPs welcomed it. It is a sector where female employment is extremely intense in processing facilities and many areas, especially as there are jobs that require precision - it was also expressed in hatcheries - and employment extends to many parts of Anatolia, to districts and even villages, to many capillaries. Therefore, it is very important, as the Commission, we will show the will to do whatever needs to be done in terms of hunting, in terms of aquaculture, in terms of other stakeholders and whatever else needs to be done to pave the way for the sector.

Of course, I would like to reiterate what I have said, especially the environment is extremely important. In case of environmental pollution in the seas or inland waters, it is not possible for this sector to exist. We talked about the aquaculture sector. It is not possible for this sector to continue if there is no water, if there is a shortage, if there is a drought, if there is pollution in the water. In this sense, the industry will perhaps move towards a new, different direction in terms of creating that sterile environment by increasing closed systems, that is, after taking water, using it within itself with various methods, and creating that sterile environment, because we see this in the field.

GNAT TV CORRESPONDENT GÜLNUR ATAŞÇİ - Thank you sir, thank you very much.

CHAIRMAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Yes, our last meeting, if there are any other valuable members of our parliament who want to speak or want to express... Mr. Kayıhan, let me ask again, who wants to speak?

VEHBİ KOÇ (Trabzon) - President, if you allow...

records.originalTurkish:
BAŞKAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Evet, teşekkür ediyoruz.

Tabii, kooperatiflerin, balıkçı barınaklarının bir kısmında mezat var. Belki bu vesileyle o mezatın artırılacağı alanların ve mezat dışında da belki dediğiniz gibi, tam büyük değil ama daha küçük ölçekli balık pişirilmesinin yapılabileceği mekânların talebi geldi sahadan da sizin ifade ettiğiniz gibi.

VEHBİ KOÇ (Trabzon) - Satış da dâhildir buna.

BAŞKAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Yani satış ve tüketim konusunda bir düzenleme yapmayı tabii, mutlaka değerlendireceğiz Komisyon olarak. Belki balıkçı barınaklarının -şu anki mevzuat itibarıyla onların yapılması mümkün değilse bile- çevresinde böyle bir sistemin oluşması söz konusu olabilir ya da değerlendirdiğimizde gerçekten, barınaklarla ilgili böyle bir süreç... İşte, normal şartlarda balıkçı barınaklarının kapısında ne yazıyor? "Üye olmayan giremez." balıkçı barınağına. Dolayısıyla, o "Üye olmayan giremez."i biraz daha -dediğiniz gibi- çevrenin de balıkçıları rahatsız etmeyecek şekilde kullanımına, en azından görmesine açmak gerektiğini ifade ettiniz.

Teşekkür ediyoruz Sayın Vehbi Koç.
records.englishTranslation:
PRESIDENT İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Yes, we thank you.

Of course, there are auctions in some of the cooperatives and fishing ports. Maybe on this occasion, there was a demand from the field for areas where that auction would be increased and, as you said, for places where fish could be cooked on a smaller scale, but not exactly on a large scale, outside the auction.

VEHBİ KOÇ (Trabzon) - This includes sales.

PRESIDENT İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - As the Commission, we will definitely consider making a regulation regarding sales and consumption. Maybe it is possible to form such a system around fishing shelters - even if it is not possible to build them under the current legislation - or when we evaluate, such a process is actually related to shelters... Here, what is written on the doors of fishing shelters under normal conditions? "Non-members cannot enter." to the fishing port. Therefore, you stated that "Non-members cannot enter" should be opened up a little more - as you said - for the use of the environment, or at least for viewing, in a way that does not disturb the fishermen.

Thank you Mr. Vehbi Koç.

records.originalTurkish:
BAŞKAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Teşekkürler Sayın Elmas.

Ben de başta Başkan Vekilimize, Sözcümüze ve bütün üyelerimize olmak üzere değerli milletvekillerimize, Komisyon toplantılarında ve saha çalışmalarında bizlerle birlikte olan bütün ekibimize, stenograflarından kameramanına, fotoğraflarımızı çekenine, lojistiğimizi sağlayan ekibimizden, Meclis personelinden uzmanlarımıza kadar hepsine, bugün burada olan siz değerli katılımcılara, danışmanlarımıza, kendi çalışma arkadaşlarımıza, emeği geçen herkese bir kez daha teşekkür ediyorum.

Balıkçılık ve Su Ürünleri Sektöründe Yaşanan Sorunların Araştırılarak Alınması Gereken Tedbirlerin Belirlenmesi Amacıyla Kurulan Meclis Araştırması Komisyonumuzun son Komisyon toplantısını kapatıyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
CHAIRMAN İSMAİL EMRAH KARAYEL - Thank you, Mr. Elmas.

I would like to thank once again, especially our Deputy Chairman, our Spokesperson and all our members, our esteemed MPs, our entire team who were with us in the Commission meetings and field studies, from our stenographers to the cameraman, from the person who took our photographs, from our team providing our logistics, from the Assembly staff to our experts, to all of you, our valued participants who are here today, our consultants, our own colleagues, and everyone who contributed.

I am closing the last Commission meeting of our Parliamentary Research Commission, which was established to investigate the problems encountered in the fisheries and aquaculture sector and determine the measures to be taken.
